task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for automated clinical diagnosis. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist of the most relevant and methodologically rigorous papers.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML method applied directly to automated clinical diagnosis (disease detection, imaging diagnosis, or differential diagnosis from patient data). Prognosis, treatment selection, drug discovery, and methodology-only papers should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague method; 3=clear method with metrics; 4=full method with baselines and ablations.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Glaucoma Detection from Fundus Photography at Population Scale
papers.abstract: We train an Inception-V4 on 101,000 fundus photographs from population-based screening to detect referable glaucoma. The model achieves AUC 0.99, sensitivity 0.97 at 99% specificity, and is validated prospectively across two independent screening cohorts. An external audit by a panel of glaucoma specialists confirms agreement on 94% of ambiguous cases. Full ablation of image preprocessing steps is reported.
papers.topic_hint: deep learning, glaucoma, fundus photography, population screening, optic disc
papers.methodology_hint: Inception-V4, 101k fundus images, AUC 0.99, prospective validation two cohorts, specialist audit, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: Inception-V4 glaucoma detector AUC 0.99, prospectively validated with specialist audit and preprocessing ablation.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for Anti-VEGF Treatment Decision Support in Wet AMD
papers.abstract: Machine learning for clinical diagnosis and decision support in ophthalmology is rapidly advancing. We apply reinforcement learning to OCT imaging time series to recommend injection intervals for anti-VEGF treatment of wet age-related macular degeneration. The RL policy reduces injection frequency by 18% versus a fixed treat-and-extend protocol in a retrospective cohort of 3,200 patients. This is a treatment selection tool.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, anti-VEGF, AMD, OCT, treatment selection
papers.methodology_hint: RL, OCT time series, 3200 patients, injection frequency reduction 18%, treatment not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: RL policy reducing anti-VEGF injection frequency by 18% vs fixed treat-and-extend protocol.

papers.title: Vision Transformer for Diabetic Macular Oedema Detection and Grading from OCT
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a ViT-L/16 on 98,000 OCT B-scans from the OCTAGON multi-site dataset to detect and grade diabetic macular oedema across three severity levels. The model achieves macro-AUC of 0.96 and outperforms ResNet-50, EfficientNet-B4, and a retinal specialist consensus. Per-layer attention analysis reveals fluid-containing hyperreflective regions as the primary diagnostic signal.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, diabetic macular oedema, OCT, grading, OCTAGON, retinal diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-L/16, OCTAGON multi-site, macro-AUC 0.96, ResNet/EfficientNet/specialist baselines, attention analysis
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for DMO grading from OCT with macro-AUC 0.96, outperforming retinal specialist consensus.

papers.title: Prognosis of Visual Acuity Loss After Cataract Surgery Using Machine Learning
papers.abstract: Machine learning for ophthalmic clinical decision support encompasses both diagnosis and prognosis applications. We apply gradient boosting to pre-operative biometry and clinical variables to predict 3-month post-operative visual acuity outcomes in 4,800 cataract surgery patients, achieving mean absolute error of 0.08 logMAR. This is a prognostic model; no diagnostic classification is performed.
papers.topic_hint: gradient boosting, cataract surgery, visual acuity prognosis, biometry
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, pre-operative biometry, MAE 0.08 logMAR, prognosis not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Gradient boosting for 3-month post-cataract visual acuity prognosis with MAE 0.08 logMAR.

papers.title: CNN for Automated Diagnosis of Age-Related Macular Degeneration Staging from Fundus
papers.abstract: We develop a convolutional neural network trained on 80,000 fundus photographs from the Age-Related Eye Disease Study to classify AMD across five AREDS stages. The model achieves AUC 0.96 and quadratic weighted kappa 0.88, outperforming two-reader grading consensus. Grad-CAM visualisations identify drusen distribution and RPE atrophy as primary staging features.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, AMD staging, AREDS, fundus photography, macular degeneration
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, AREDS 80k images, AUC 0.96, kappa 0.88, grading consensus comparison, Grad-CAM
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for five-stage AMD classification with AUC 0.96, outperforming reader consensus with Grad-CAM.

papers.title: Drug Repurposing for Ophthalmic Conditions Using Knowledge Graph Embeddings
papers.abstract: We apply knowledge graph embedding methods to identify drug repurposing candidates for rare ophthalmic conditions including choroideremia and Stargardt disease. Using 1.2 million biomedical knowledge graph triples, link prediction identifies 18 plausible drug-disease candidates not in the training data. No clinical diagnostic model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: knowledge graph, drug repurposing, ophthalmic disease, choroideremia, link prediction
papers.methodology_hint: KG embedding, link prediction, 1.2M triples, drug repurposing candidates, no diagnostic model
papers.claimed_contribution: Knowledge graph embedding identifying 18 drug repurposing candidates for rare ophthalmic conditions.

papers.title: Attention U-Net for Automated Optic Disc and Cup Segmentation in Glaucoma Assessment
papers.abstract: We train an attention U-Net on 4,500 fundus photographs to segment the optic disc and cup for automated cup-to-disc ratio measurement supporting glaucoma assessment. Dice scores of 0.96 and 0.87 are achieved for disc and cup respectively. The model outputs CDR values that correlate r=0.93 with manual grading. Three publicly available fundus segmentation benchmarks are included in evaluation.
papers.topic_hint: attention U-Net, optic disc segmentation, cup-to-disc ratio, glaucoma, fundus
papers.methodology_hint: attention U-Net, 4500 fundus, Dice 0.96/0.87, CDR r=0.93, three benchmark evaluation
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention U-Net for optic disc/cup segmentation achieving Dice 0.96/0.87 with CDR correlation r=0.93.

papers.title: Survey of Deep Learning Methods for Retinal Fundus Image Analysis
papers.abstract: We survey 230 papers on deep learning methods for automated analysis of retinal fundus images, covering diabetic retinopathy grading, glaucoma detection, AMD classification, and vessel segmentation. We present a benchmark comparison of leading architectures and identify open challenges in domain generalisation and uncertainty quantification. No new model is proposed.
papers.topic_hint: survey, fundus image analysis, deep learning, retinal diagnosis, benchmark comparison
papers.methodology_hint: survey of 230 papers, benchmark comparison, no new model, architecture review
papers.claimed_contribution: Survey of 230 fundus deep learning papers with benchmark comparison and open challenge analysis.

papers.title: Self-Supervised Pre-training for Low-Resource Retinal Disease Classification
papers.abstract: We apply momentum contrastive pre-training on 2 million unlabelled fundus photographs to learn retinal representations. The pre-trained encoder improves AUC by 8-12% on four downstream retinal disease classification tasks compared to supervised baselines when fine-tuned with 100 labels. Ablation of pre-training dataset size and augmentation strategy is provided.
papers.topic_hint: self-supervised learning, fundus pre-training, retinal disease, contrastive, low-resource
papers.methodology_hint: MoCo pre-training, 2M unlabelled fundus, AUC improvement 8-12%, four tasks, ablation of dataset size
papers.claimed_contribution: Contrastive fundus pre-training improving downstream retinal disease AUC 8-12% with 100 labels.

papers.title: Federated Learning for Multi-Site Diabetic Retinopathy Grading Without Data Sharing
papers.abstract: We implement federated learning across 6 diabetic retinopathy screening sites in three countries to train a DR grading model without centralising patient fundus images. The federated model achieves AUC 0.93, within 2% of the centralised upper bound. We note this is a federated training methodology paper; the diagnostic model itself builds on published DR grading architectures.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, diabetic retinopathy, multi-site, privacy, grading methodology
papers.methodology_hint: federated training, 6 sites three countries, AUC 0.93, 2% below centralised, methodology paper
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated training achieving AUC 0.93 for DR grading across 6 international sites without data sharing.

papers.title: Sparse Autoencoder for Anomaly Detection in OCT Volume Scans
papers.abstract: We train a sparse autoencoder on 50,000 healthy OCT volume scans and use reconstruction error as an anomaly score to detect unseen pathologies including macular holes, epiretinal membranes, and vitreous detachment. The approach achieves AUC 0.88 across five pathology classes without class-specific supervision, outperforming density estimation and one-class SVM baselines.
papers.topic_hint: sparse autoencoder, OCT, anomaly detection, macular hole, retinal pathology
papers.methodology_hint: sparse autoencoder, 50k healthy OCTs, reconstruction error, AUC 0.88, five pathology classes, one-class SVM baseline
papers.claimed_contribution: Sparse autoencoder OCT anomaly detector achieving AUC 0.88 across five pathologies without class supervision.

papers.title: Automated Intraocular Pressure Prediction for Glaucoma Risk Stratification from Fundus
papers.abstract: Machine learning for ophthalmic clinical decision support includes novel biomarker estimation. We train a CNN to predict intraocular pressure from fundus photographs as a surrogate biomarker for glaucoma risk stratification. Pearson correlation 0.71 with tonometry measurements is achieved. IOP prediction supports risk stratification; automated glaucoma diagnosis is not performed.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, intraocular pressure, fundus photography, glaucoma risk, biomarker prediction
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, IOP prediction from fundus, Pearson 0.71, risk stratification not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN predicting IOP from fundus photographs with Pearson 0.71 for glaucoma risk stratification.

papers.title: Multi-Modal CNN for Conjunctival Melanoma Diagnosis from Anterior Segment Photography
papers.abstract: We develop a multi-modal CNN that fuses anterior segment photography and patient metadata to classify conjunctival lesions as benign, dysplastic, or malignant melanoma. Trained on 2,800 lesions from four tertiary referral centres, the model achieves AUC 0.94 and sensitivity 0.91 at 85% specificity. Comparison against three consultant ophthalmologists and a dermoscopy baseline is provided.
papers.topic_hint: multi-modal CNN, conjunctival melanoma, anterior segment photography, ocular oncology, diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: multi-modal CNN, 2800 conjunctival lesions, AUC 0.94, sensitivity 0.91, ophthalmologist comparison, dermoscopy baseline
papers.claimed_contribution: Multi-modal CNN for conjunctival melanoma diagnosis with AUC 0.94, validated against specialist ophthalmologists.

papers.title: LSTM for Predicting Visual Field Progression Rate in Glaucoma
papers.abstract: Machine learning for ophthalmic clinical decision support supports treatment monitoring. We apply an LSTM to longitudinal series of visual field Humphrey 24-2 tests from 1,800 glaucoma patients to predict mean deviation progression rate over the next 2 years, achieving mean absolute error of 0.8 dB/year. Progression rate prediction informs treatment escalation decisions. This is a prognosis model.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, visual field, glaucoma progression, Humphrey 24-2, prognosis
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM, longitudinal visual fields, 1800 patients, MAE 0.8 dB/year, prognosis not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM for glaucoma visual field progression rate prediction with MAE 0.8 dB/year.

papers.title: Explainable AI for Retinopathy of Prematurity Staging from Retinal Imaging
papers.abstract: We train a DenseNet-121 on 6,000 retinal images from premature infants to classify retinopathy of prematurity at five international classification stages. The model achieves AUC 0.95 and quadratic weighted kappa 0.87, outperforming three neonatologist graders. LIME and Grad-CAM provide lesion-level explanations that improve clinician trust in a simulation study.
papers.topic_hint: DenseNet, retinopathy of prematurity, ROP staging, neonatal retinal imaging, explainability
papers.methodology_hint: DenseNet-121, 6000 neonatal retinal images, AUC 0.95, kappa 0.87, neonatologist comparison, LIME+Grad-CAM
papers.claimed_contribution: DenseNet for ROP staging with AUC 0.95 and LIME/Grad-CAM explanations improving clinician trust.

papers.title: Uncertainty Quantification in Ophthalmic Deep Learning: A Methodology Study
papers.abstract: We evaluate Monte Carlo dropout, deep ensembles, and conformal prediction for uncertainty quantification in three ophthalmic image classification tasks. No new diagnostic model is developed; this is a methodology comparison aimed at guiding uncertainty-aware deployment of existing retinal classifiers.
papers.topic_hint: uncertainty quantification, Monte Carlo dropout, ensembles, ophthalmic imaging, methodology
papers.methodology_hint: MC dropout, deep ensembles, conformal prediction, three tasks, methodology comparison only
papers.claimed_contribution: Comparison of uncertainty quantification methods for ophthalmic image classifiers across three tasks.

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Network for Synthetic Retinal Image Augmentation
papers.abstract: We train a conditional GAN on fundus photographs to generate synthetic retinal images with specified pathology labels for dataset augmentation. Classifiers trained with GAN augmentation improve AUC by 5-7% for four rare retinal conditions. We note this is an augmentation methodology paper; the downstream diagnostic models use published architectures.
papers.topic_hint: GAN, synthetic data augmentation, fundus, retinal disease, dataset imbalance
papers.methodology_hint: conditional GAN, fundus augmentation, AUC improvement 5-7%, augmentation methodology paper
papers.claimed_contribution: Conditional GAN for synthetic fundus augmentation improving rare retinal disease AUC 5-7%.

papers.title: Causal Inference for Identifying Risk Factors of Myopia Progression in Children
papers.abstract: We apply doubly-robust causal inference to a longitudinal study of 12,000 children to identify causal risk factors for myopia progression, including outdoor exposure, screen time, and parental refractive error. No predictive model or diagnostic system is developed; this is an observational causal study.
papers.topic_hint: causal inference, myopia progression, longitudinal cohort, risk factors, children
papers.methodology_hint: doubly-robust causal inference, 12k children, risk factor identification, observational study, no diagnostic model
papers.claimed_contribution: Causal identification of myopia progression risk factors in 12,000 children using doubly-robust inference.

papers.title: EfficientNet for Corneal Pathology Classification from Anterior Segment OCT
papers.abstract: We fine-tune an EfficientNet-B5 on 18,000 anterior segment OCT scans to classify six corneal pathologies including keratoconus, Fuchs dystrophy, and bullous keratopathy. The model achieves macro-F1 of 0.91 and AUC 0.95, outperforming ResNet-50, VGG-16, and a slit-lamp expert grader baseline. Per-class analysis identifies keratoconus as the highest-performance category.
papers.topic_hint: EfficientNet, corneal pathology, anterior segment OCT, keratoconus, diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet-B5, 18k AS-OCT, macro-F1 0.91, AUC 0.95, ResNet/VGG/expert baselines, per-class analysis
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet for six-class corneal pathology diagnosis from AS-OCT with macro-F1 0.91 and AUC 0.95.

papers.title: Physics-Informed Neural Network for Intraocular Lens Power Calculation
papers.abstract: We embed optical refraction physics into a neural network to improve intraocular lens power calculation for cataract surgery planning. The model reduces prediction error for post-operative target refraction from 0.41D (existing formula) to 0.28D mean absolute error. This is a surgical planning tool, not a diagnostic classifier.
papers.topic_hint: physics-informed NN, IOL calculation, cataract surgery, refraction, surgical planning
papers.methodology_hint: physics-informed NN, IOL power, MAE 0.28D, optical formula comparison, surgical planning not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Physics-informed NN for IOL power calculation reducing prediction error from 0.41D to 0.28D MAE.
